launch_cutline.description: A go/no-go rehearsal stage renders a Go verdict for a Stable (or LTS) public claim only when it was rehearsed end-to-end behind a captured, within-freshness-SLO rehearsal packet, every required rollback checkpoint was verified to a restore point, the stage owner has signed, any exception packet it relies on is unexpired, and the public claim it backs is itself at or above the cutline. A stage whose rehearsal packet breached its freshness SLO or is missing, whose rollback checkpoint is unverified, whose evidence is incomplete, whose owner sign-off is missing, whose exception packet expired, or whose backing public claim is below the cutline narrows to a No-Go below the cutline (beta, preview, withdrawn) and never inherits an adjacent rehearsed stage's verdict.

rules.title: Backing public claim narrowed below cutline
rules.rationale: A rehearsal stage whose backing public claim is already below the stable cutline inherits that ceiling; the stable claim manifest holds the claim upstream, so this is expected inheritance rather than a rehearsal failure and does not hold the go/no-go on its own.

rules.title: Rehearsal evidence incomplete
rules.rationale: A rehearsal stage whose dress-rehearsal evidence is incomplete cannot return a Go for a Stable public claim; it narrows and the go/no-go holds until the evidence is recaptured.

rules.title: Rehearsal packet breached its freshness SLO
rules.rationale: A rehearsal stage whose rehearsal packet has aged past its freshness SLO no longer proves the stage was exercised on the current build; it narrows and the go/no-go holds until the packet is refreshed within its SLO.

rules.title: Rehearsal packet missing
rules.rationale: A rehearsal stage with no captured rehearsal packet cannot return a Go for a Stable public claim by riding an adjacent rehearsed stage; it narrows and the go/no-go holds until a packet is captured and signed off.

rules.title: Relied-on exception packet expired
rules.rationale: A rehearsal stage that returned Go only because of an exception packet loses that authorization once the exception expires; it narrows and the go/no-go holds until the underlying gap is closed or a fresh exception packet is granted.

rules.title: Stage owner signoff missing
rules.rationale: A rehearsal stage cannot return a Go for a Stable public claim without the stage owner's signoff; it narrows and the go/no-go holds until the owner signs.

rules.title: Required rollback checkpoint unverified
rules.rationale: A rehearsal stage with a required rollback checkpoint that was not verified to a restore point cannot return a Go for a Stable public claim; it narrows and the go/no-go holds until the checkpoint is verified.

rows.title: Explicit launch-cutline go/no-go signoff
rows.subject_summary: The final review that fixes the explicit launch cutline and confirms which surfaces sit at or above Stable versus narrowed below it before the train is allowed to promote.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Surfaces below the cutline confirmed narrowed

rows.rationale: A captured, within-SLO rehearsal packet, a frozen cutline checkpoint, and a confirmed narrowed-surfaces checkpoint back the explicit launch-cutline signoff at the repair claim's Stable label.

rows.title: Promotion publish-step dry run
rows.subject_summary: The end-to-end dry run of the promotion publish step from the release candidate through the publish targets, with the gate map exercised against the live train.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Promotion dry run completed against the live train

rows.rationale: The promotion publish-step dry run rides a fresh packet, a verified dry-run checkpoint, and an owner sign-off, so it returns Go for the provider claim's Stable label.

rows.title: Live-hardware rollback checkpoint drill
rows.subject_summary: The live-hardware rollback drill that restores the previous build from a verified rollback checkpoint and confirms the workspace returns to a clean known-good state.

exception_packet.reason: The live-hardware rollback drill restored the primary target from a verified checkpoint; an active exception packet authorizes the stage to return Go at the repair claim's Stable label while the same drill on one secondary hardware target is still being captured.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Previous build restored from a verified checkpoint
Workspace returned to a clean known-good state

rows.rationale: The live-hardware rollback drill restored the primary target from a verified checkpoint and rides a fresh packet; an active, unexpired exception packet covers the secondary-target drill, so the stage returns Go at the repair claim's Stable label on exception.

rows.title: Rollback state-integrity checkpoint drill
rows.subject_summary: The drill that proves a rollback preserves on-disk state and history integrity, restoring the project state to the pre-update checkpoint without data loss.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Project state restored to the pre-update checkpoint without loss
History integrity preserved across the rollback

rows.rationale: The state-integrity drill rides a fresh packet, but the history-integrity rollback checkpoint was not verified, so the checkpoint automation narrows the stage to a No-Go at beta and holds the go/no-go until the checkpoint is verified.

rows.title: Promotion roll-forward recovery rehearsal
rows.subject_summary: The rehearsal of the roll-forward path that recovers a stalled promotion by re-driving the publish step rather than rolling back, exercised against the live train.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Stalled promotion recovered by re-driving the publish step

rows.rationale: The roll-forward recovery rehearsal aged past its freshness SLO against as_of, so it no longer proves the path works on the current build; it narrows to a No-Go at beta and holds the go/no-go until the rehearsal is re-run even though its checkpoint still held.

rows.title: Open exception-packet review
rows.subject_summary: The review that confirms every open launch exception packet is recorded, owner-attributed, time-boxed with an expiry, and tied to a tracked closure plan before the go/no-go.

exception_packet.reason: An exception packet authorized returning Go while one residual readiness-lane gap was being closed; the exception packet has since expired against as_of.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Every open exception packet recorded and time-boxed

rows.rationale: The exception-packet review returned Go at Stable only on an exception packet that has expired against as_of; the exception-expiry automation narrows it to a No-Go at beta and holds the go/no-go until the residual gap is closed without an exception.

rows.title: Export and offboarding promotion dry run
rows.subject_summary: The dry run that rehearses promoting and offboarding an export bundle to the open-source build, validating the export path end to end.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Export and offboarding path validated end to end

rows.rationale: The export and offboarding promotion dry run rides a fresh packet, a verified checkpoint, and an owner sign-off, but its backing public claim is published beta, so it inherits that ceiling and renders a beta verdict rather than widening to Stable.

rows.title: Regulated-environment exception review
rows.subject_summary: The review of the open exception packets governing regulated-environment assurance claims before the go/no-go.

rollback_checkpoints.title: Regulated-environment exception packets recorded and time-boxed

rows.rationale: The regulated-environment exception review carries a fresh, signed-off packet with a verified checkpoint, but its backing public claim is published beta, so it inherits that ceiling and renders a beta verdict rather than widening to Stable.

publication.rationale: Go/no-go holds: a rollback state-integrity stage with an unverified history-integrity checkpoint, a roll-forward recovery rehearsal whose packet breached its freshness SLO, and an exception-packet review whose exception expired all narrow to No-Go below the cutline under public claims that are still published Stable.
